title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa describes how to build and scale AI agents in production across thousands of government workflows. The talk covers agent architecture patterns including core loops, feedback mechanisms, observability, and tooling approaches used in their OG Assist product.
writeup: This talk from OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa covers building and scaling AI agents in production across thousands of government workflows, drawing on the OG Assist product. It walks through agent architecture patterns: core loops, feedback mechanisms, observability, and tooling approaches. Related concepts include the A2A protocol, evals, and long-context handling. The relevance is practical: it grounds agent design in a live deployment serving government use cases rather than in prototypes.
